report_name: EU Launches Action Plan to Balance AI's Role in Cybersecurity

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: The executive branch of the European Union, which introduced the Action Plan on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence to foster safe AI use in cyber defense and build resilience against AI-powered threats.

identities.name: EU Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA)
identities.description: The EU agency tasked with a central role in developing blueprints for secure AI access and establishing testing platforms under the new Action Plan for AI and Cybersecurity.

identities.name: EU Member States
identities.description: National governments within the European Union that are expected to align their national cybersecurity strategies with the objectives of the EU's Action Plan on AI and Cybersecurity.

identities.name: Technology Companies
identities.description: A class of stakeholders, including developers of advanced AI models and cybersecurity solution providers, who will be affected by new evaluation requirements and innovation opportunities under the EU's Action Plan.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure Operators
identities.description: Entities covered by the NIS2 Directive that will be encouraged and guided to adopt AI-powered defensive tools as part of the EU's Action Plan on AI and Cybersecurity.

identities.name: Research and Academia
identities.description: A class of stakeholders who will have new funding and collaboration opportunities for research into AI and cybersecurity through initiatives like the 'EU Grand Challenge'.
